basic_details.job_title: CERT-In Scientist-B Recruitment 2026: 133 Vacancies, GATE-Based Selection, Apply Online
basic_details.selection_process_brief: GATE Score shortlisting, Document Verification, Personal Interaction/Interview
basic_details.job_location: Anywhere in India or outside India
basic_details.short_information: The Indian Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT-In), functioning under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), has invited online applications for 133 Scientist-'B' posts (equivalent to Group-'A') on a direct recruitment basis. Shortlisting for the Personal Interaction/Interview stage will be based on a valid GATE score of 2024, 2025, or 2026. Eligible candidates can apply online from 14 July 2026 to 17 August 2026 (5:30 PM).

important_dates.interview_date: Not yet released - will be announced on the CERT-In website

application_fee.details: The application fee is Nil for all categories - no fee payment is required for this recruitment.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as completed years on the cut-off date (17.08.2026). Additional age relaxation of 5 years applies to candidates serving in Government Organizations in the same or allied cadre where prior service would be useful for the advertised post. Age relaxation for PwBD candidates applies irrespective of whether the post is reserved for them, provided the post is identified as suitable.

qualification.details: Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or Technology (minimum four-year course) in disciplines such as Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Software Systems, Cyber Security, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Information Security, Digital Forensics, IoT, Quantum Computing, Blockchain Technology, Machine Learning, Cloud Security, Cryptography, or any other relevant discipline where the qualifying degree names Computer Science/IT/Data Science/AI as the main discipline, OR Master's Degree in Science disciplines such as Computer Science, Computer Application, or Computer and Network Security, OR Master's in Computer Application (MCA) | Master's Degree in Science disciplines including Software System, Software Engineering, Information Technology, Informatics, Information System, Information Security, Cyber Law, Computational Linguistics, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Cyber Security, Advance Network, Cryptography, Machine Learning, Cloud Security, Digital Forensics, Big Data Analytics, IoT, Quantum Computing, or Blockchain Technology. | Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or Technology (minimum four-year course) in Electronics, Electronics & Communication, Electronics & Telecommunication, Electronics & Instrumentation, Mechatronics, Robotics and Automation, Drone Technology, Autonomous Systems, or any relevant discipline where "Electronics" is the main discipline, OR Master's Degree in Science disciplines such as Electronics, Applied Electronics, VLSI Design, Digital Communication, Mechatronics, Robotics and Automation, Drone Technology, or Autonomous Systems | The essential qualification must be obtained on or before the cut-off date. No claim of an "equivalent" qualification will be entertained. No prior work experience is required for this post.

salary_details.details: The post of Scientist-'B' carries Level 10 of the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC), with a pay scale of Rs. 56,100 to Rs. 1,77,500. Details of allowances (DA, HRA, TA) are not specified in the notification.

selection_process: GATE Score Shortlisting: Candidates are shortlisted for interview in the ratio of 1:5 (discipline-wise and category-wise)

based on GATE score merit. All candidates with a tied score at the cut-off rank are shortlisted. Document Verification: Conducted before the interaction/interview. Candidates who fail to appear with original documents are not eligible for further stages. Personal Interaction/Interview: Maximum 50 marks
and Academic performance (0.05 mark per percentage in the qualifying degree).
Candidates who do not meet the minimum qualifying marks in the interview will not be considered for the final merit list
Higher GATE paper score ranks higher If GATE scores are equal
higher marks in the Technical (Academic) knowledge section of the interview ranks higher If still tied
how_to_apply: Visit the official CERT-In website: https://www.cert-in.org.in/
Step 1 - Registration: Register with a valid email ID and active mobile number
Step 2 - Fill Online Application Form: Complete the application and upload all required documents
Step 3 - Download Confirmation Page: Submit the form finally and download the Confirmation Page - the application is treated as complete only after this step
No changes to name, category, address, mobile number, or email ID are permitted after submission. Aadhaar-based authentication will be used at every stage of the recruitment process.

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: A total of 133 vacancies have been notified across three disciplines. The number of advertised posts is tentative and may increase or decrease at the sole discretion of CERT-In. *Note: SC and OBC(NCL) figures for CS and DA disciplines include backlog vacancies - 1 SC and 3 OBC backlog post in CS, and OBC(NCL) backlog in DA (exact backlog count for DA not specified beyond the note).

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: A citizen of India, or A subject of Nepal, or A subject of Bhutan, or A Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India, or A person of Indian origin migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or specified East African countries with the intention of permanently settling in India (certificate of eligibility required for categories other than Indian citizens) Candidates must possess a valid GATE score from 2024, 2025, or 2026 in the paper code corresponding to their discipline. Equal weightage is given to GATE 2024, 2025, and 2026 scores.

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or Technology (minimum four-year course) in disciplines such as Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Software Systems, Cyber Security, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Information Security, Digital Forensics, IoT, Quantum Computing, Blockchain Technology, Machine Learning, Cloud Security, Cryptography, or any other relevant discipline where the qualifying degree names Computer Science/IT/Data Science/AI as the main discipline, OR Master's Degree in Science disciplines such as Computer Science, Computer Application, or Computer and Network Security, OR Master's in Computer Application (MCA) Master's Degree in Science disciplines including Software System, Software Engineering, Information Technology, Informatics, Information System, Information Security, Cyber Law, Computational Linguistics, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Cyber Security, Advance Network, Cryptography, Machine Learning, Cloud Security, Digital Forensics, Big Data Analytics, IoT, Quantum Computing, or Blockchain Technology. Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or Technology (minimum four-year course) in Electronics, Electronics & Communication, Electronics & Telecommunication, Electronics & Instrumentation, Mechatronics, Robotics and Automation, Drone Technology, Autonomous Systems, or any relevant discipline where "Electronics" is the main discipline, OR Master's Degree in Science disciplines such as Electronics, Applied Electronics, VLSI Design, Digital Communication, Mechatronics, Robotics and Automation, Drone Technology, or Autonomous Systems The essential qualification must be obtained on or before the cut-off date. No claim of an "equivalent" qualification will be entertained. No prior work experience is required for this post.

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Age Limit 2026
content_sections.text: Age is calculated as completed years on the cut-off date (17.08.2026). Additional age relaxation of 5 years applies to candidates serving in Government Organizations in the same or allied cadre where prior service would be useful for the advertised post. Age relaxation for PwBD candidates applies irrespective of whether the post is reserved for them, provided the post is identified as suitable.

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Salary 2026
content_sections.text: The post of Scientist-'B' carries Level 10 of the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC) , with a pay scale of Rs. 56,100 to Rs. 1,77,500 . Details of allowances (DA, HRA, TA) are not specified in the notification.

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee is Nil for all categories - no fee payment is required for this recruitment.

content_sections.heading: CERT-In Scientist-B Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: GATE Score Shortlisting: Candidates are shortlisted for interview in the ratio of 1:5 (discipline-wise and category-wise), based on GATE score merit. All candidates with a tied score at the cut-off rank are shortlisted. Document Verification: Conducted before the interaction/interview. Candidates who fail to appear with original documents are not eligible for further stages. Personal Interaction/Interview: Maximum 50 marks, assessed on Technical (Academic) knowledge, Presentation/Communication Skill/Comprehension, and Academic performance (0.05 mark per percentage in the qualifying degree). Unreserved (UR): 50% EWS/OBC/SC/ST/PwBD: 40% Candidates who do not meet the minimum qualifying marks in the interview will not be considered for the final merit list, irrespective of GATE score. Higher GATE paper score ranks higher If GATE scores are equal, higher marks in the Technical (Academic) knowledge section of the interview ranks higher If still tied, the older candidate ranks higher

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for CERT-In Scientist-B
content_sections.text: Recent coloured photograph (JPEG/JPG, 50KB–200KB) Scanned signature in blue or black ink (JPEG/JPG, 10KB–50KB) Date of Birth proof (Municipality certificate or Matriculation/High School/SSC certificate) Photo ID proof (Aadhaar, PAN, Driving Licence, EPIC, or other government ID) Matriculation and Senior Secondary certificates GATE Score Card (2024/2025/2026, as applicable) Essential qualification certificate and marksheet CGPA conversion certificate (if applicable) Category certificate - SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/EWS (if applicable) PwBD certificate with minimum 40% permanent disability (if applicable) Experience certificate (if any) No Objection Certificate - NOC (if applicable, per Annexure VII) Age relaxation certificate (if applicable, per Annexure VIII or IX) All documents must be self-attested, legible when printed, and not password-protected.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for CERT-In Scientist-B 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official CERT-In website: https://www.cert-in.org.in/ Step 1 - Registration: Register with a valid email ID and active mobile number Step 2 - Fill Online Application Form: Complete the application and upload all required documents Step 3 - Download Confirmation Page: Submit the form finally and download the Confirmation Page - the application is treated as complete only after this step No changes to name, category, address, mobile number, or email ID are permitted after submission. Aadhaar-based authentication will be used at every stage of the recruitment process.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in CERT-In Scientist-B Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 133 vacancies are notified across three disciplines: Computer Science and IT (83), Data Science and AI (25), and Electronics and Communication Engineering (25).

faq.question: Q2. Is GATE score mandatory for CERT-In Scientist-B recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates must have a valid GATE score from 2024, 2025, or 2026 in the paper code matching their discipline (CS, DA, or EC).

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for CERT-In Scientist-B 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Nil for all categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary of CERT-In Scientist-B?
faq.answer: The post carries Level 10 (7th CPC) pay of Rs. 56,100 to Rs. 1,77,500 per month.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for CERT-In Scientist-B 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 30 years for UR/EWS, 35 years for SC/ST, and 33 years for OBC(NCL), as on the cut-off date of 17.08.2026, with further relaxation for PwBD and service candidates.

faq.question: Q6. What is the last date to apply for CERT-In Scientist-B 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 17 August 2026 at 5:30 PM.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for CERT-In Scientist-B?
faq.answer: Selection involves GATE score-based shortlisting (1:5 ratio), Document Verification, and a Personal Interaction/Interview, with final merit based on 60% weightage to GATE score and 40% weightage to the interview.

faq.question: Q8. Is prior work experience required for CERT-In Scientist-B post?
faq.answer: No. The notification specifies "No Experience Required" for this post.

faq.question: Q9. Where will the CERT-In Scientist-B interview be conducted?
faq.answer: Interactions/Interviews will be conducted at Delhi or at any other location/mode approved by the competent authority.
